Question: Use Cramer’s Rule to solve the 2 × 2 system of equations. $$\left .\begin{matrix}{a}\text{  } x + 2 y = -11 \\ - 2 x + y = -13\end{matrix}\right .$$
Find the determinant of the 3 × 3 matrix. $$\det{(} A ) = \left |\begin{matrix}1 & - 3 & 7 \\ 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 1 & - 2 & 3\end{matrix}\right |$$
Use Cramer’s Rule to solve the 3 × 3 matrix. $$\left .\begin{matrix}{a}x - 3 y + 7 z = 13 \\ x + y + z = 1 \\ x - 2 y + 3 z = 4\end{matrix}\right .$$
